items.title: Neuf «faux policiers» arrêtés après un spectaculaire cambriolage à Dakar
items.description: La Police nationale sénégalaise a annoncé, ce lundi 20 juillet 2026 sur sa page Facebook, le démantèlement d'un réseau criminel spécialisé dans les cambriolages avec violence, les séquestrations et les vols aggravés. Neuf personnes qui se faisaient passer pour des «officiers de police» ont été arrêtées après une enquête menée par la Sûreté urbaine de Dakar à la suite d'un braquage visant deux ressortissants indiens à Fann-Hock. La Sûreté urbaine du Commissariat central de Dakar a porté un coup important à la criminalité organisée en procédant à l'arrestation de neuf membres présumés d'un réseau spécialisé dans les cambriolages avec violence, les séquestrations et les vols aggravés. L'information a été rendue publique ce lundi 20 juillet 2026 par la Police nationale sénégalaise sur sa page Facebook officielle. Les faits remontent au 25 juin 2026. Aux environs de 14 heures, plusieurs individus se sont présentés dans un appartement situé dans le quartier Fann-Hock en se faisant passer pour des officiers de police. Pour convaincre leurs victimes, ils ont exhibé une fausse carte professionnelle ainsi qu'un faux mandat de perquisition, affirmant mener une opération visant des ressortissants indiens soupçonnés de blanchiment d'argent provenant du trafic de stupéfiants. Après avoir fouillé les lieux, les malfaiteurs se sont emparés d'une somme de 50 millions de F CFA cachée dans une armoire. Ils ont ensuite menotté les deux occupants de l'appartement avant de les contraindre à monter dans un véhicule. Les victimes ont été retenues contre leur gré et transportées à travers plusieurs quartiers de Dakar pendant près de trois heures avant d'être abandonnées sur la VDN 3, sous la menace. Dès la saisine du dossier, la Sûreté urbaine a engagé des investigations approfondies. L'exploitation des images de vidéosurveillance a permis d'identifier le véhicule utilisé lors de l'opération, tandis que la géolocalisation et l'analyse des données téléphoniques ont confirmé la présence des suspects sur le parcours décrit par les victimes. Les investigations ont conduit à l'interpellation de neuf personnes, parmi lesquelles figure le propriétaire et conducteur du véhicule ayant servi à l'opération. Selon la police, les suspects ont reconnu leur implication dans les faits, au cours des auditions. L'enquête a également révélé que les membres du réseau préparaient leurs opérations dans un mini-restaurant situé à « Case Ba », appartenant à l'un d'eux. C'est dans ce lieu que les rôles étaient répartis et que des hommes de main étaient recrutés pour exécuter les cambriolages. Les enquêteurs ont, par ailleurs, révélé que le coup avait été facilité par des informations fournies par un ancien collaborateur des victimes, qui travaillait avec elles dans le secteur du change et qui a été formellement identifié. Les perquisitions effectuées aux domiciles des suspects ont permis de saisir les vêtements utilisés lors du cambriolage, un équipement militaire comprenant notamment une tenue de gendarmerie, un béret, un ceinturon et un insigne, les menottes ayant servi à immobiliser les victimes, ainsi qu'une partie de l'argent provenant du partage du butin. La Police nationale sénégalaise indique que les investigations se poursuivent afin de retrouver trois autres membres présumés de cette organisation criminelle, toujours en fuite. Les autorités entendent également faire toute la lumière sur les ramifications de ce réseau qui usait de fausses qualités d'agents des forces de sécurité pour commettre ses forfaits. Par Valentin SOMANDE L’article Neuf «faux policiers» arrêtés après un spectaculaire cambriolage à Dakar est apparu en premier sur Wakat Séra .

items.title: Poni: les acteurs locaux réfléchissent à la gestion des personnes déplacées internes et à l’Assurance maladie universelle
items.description: Burkina-Poni-CCP-Situation-Humanitaire Poni: les acteurs locaux réfléchissent à la gestion des personnes déplacées internes et à l'Assurance maladie universelle Gaoua, 17 juil. 2026 (AIB) – Le Haut-commissaire de la province du Poni, Bepampo Ouoba, a présidé, vendredi à Gaoua, les travaux de la première session du Cadre de concertation provinciale (CCP) placée sous le thème « Gestion des personnes déplacées internes dans la province du Poni et Assurance maladie universelle ». La rencontre a réuni les représentants de l'administration publique, des collectivités territoriales, des services techniques déconcentrés, des projets et programmes ainsi que des organisations de la société civile autour des principaux enjeux humanitaires et sociaux de la province. Dans son discours d'ouverture, le Haut-commissaire a indiqué que le Cadre de concertation provinciale constitue un espace de dialogue et de coordination des interventions des acteurs du développement local. Il a rappelé que cette instance permet de partager les informations utiles à la prise de décision et de capitaliser les bonnes pratiques au profit des populations. Les participants ont suivi trois communications portant sur la situation humanitaire dans la province du Poni, la situation alimentaire et nutritionnelle ainsi que le régime de l'Assurance maladie universelle, suivies d'échanges. Selon le Haut-commissaire, cette session est consacrée notamment à l'examen de la situation des personnes déplacées internes, particulièrement celles ayant regagné leurs localités d'origine grâce aux efforts consentis par l'État et les Forces de défense et de sécurité dans la reconquête du territoire national. Les travaux ont également permis de faire le point de la campagne agricole 2026-2027 et de la situation alimentaire et nutritionnelle dans la province, en vue d'apprécier la disponibilité des denrées alimentaires et d'encourager les producteurs agricoles pour une campagne réussie. Le coordonnateur des programmes de l'association Semfilms, Jean Sylvanus Ouali a indiqué que son organisation accompagne cette activité dans le cadre de la Caravane de dialogue pour la paix et la cohésion sociale dans le Sud du Burkina Faso. Il a précisé que cette initiative comprend l'animation des cadres de concertation provinciaux du Poni et de la Sissili ainsi que l'organisation de soirées culturelles de sensibilisation à Léo, Dano, Gaoua et Batié. Selon lui, ces activités visent à promouvoir le vivre-ensemble, le dialogue communautaire et la gestion apaisée des conflits, en particulier les conflits fonciers. À travers des projections cinématographiques et des animations socioculturelles, Semfilms entend renforcer la cohésion sociale et sensibiliser les populations aux mécanismes de prévention et de résolution pacifique des différends liés à l'accès et à la gestion des terres. À l'issue de cette première session, les organisateurs ont souhaité que les recommandations formulées contribuent à renforcer la coordination des actions humanitaires et sociales dans la province du Poni et à améliorer les conditions de vie des populations. Agence d'Information du Burkina MS/bak

items.title: Who is Hamas' new leader Khalil Al-Hayya?
items.description: By Nidal al-Mughrabi July 20 (Reuters) - Khalil Al-Hayya, a senior Hamas official who survived an Israeli raid on Qatar in September last year and who is seen as having good ties with Iran, was named on Monday as the overall leader of the Palestinian militant group. Hayya, who lost a son in the Gaza conflict and two sons in previous wars, has been based with other Hamas leaders in Doha.

items.title: Port autonome de Conakry : Ousmane Gaoual met les pieds dans le plat et mise sur les ports périphériques
items.description: Le ministre des Transports et porte-parole du Gouvernement, Ousmane Gaoual Diallo, n’a pas cherché à masquer la vérité ce lundi 20 juillet 2026. Invité à réagir sur le classement peu envieux du Port autonome de Conakry, épinglé parmi les moins performants de la planète, il a reconnu les dysfonctionnements tout en voulant rassurer sur une embellie en cours. « C’est une accumulation de mauvaises pratiques, d'une faiblesse dans les investissements et d'un manque d'anticipation dans la politique publique du secteur », a asséné le ministre au micro de nos confrères de Télé 24. Une autocritique rare, balayant d'un revers de main la langue de bois. Il assure toutefois que la bête commence à être domptée, les délais d'attente des navires sur rade étant passés d'un mois, voire un mois et demi, à des attentes plus raisonnables de « cinq ou six jours ». Décongestionner par la périphérie Pour éviter l'asphyxie chronique de la plus grande infrastructure portuaire du pays, l’exécutif joue la carte de l'éclatement géographique. Le plan de bataille passe par l'aménagement et la montée en puissance des ports périphériques de Dubréka, Moribaya et Kamsar. Le principe ? Transborder la marchandise en amont pour soulager les quais de la capitale. « Ça va réduire le coût du transfert des conteneurs en utilisant de petites barges pouvant transporter de 40 à 200 conteneurs pour les acheminer d'ici vers Dubréka, Moribaya et Kamsar, afin que ces endroits soient les lieux où l'on va dépoter, dédouaner et disperser la marchandise », détaille le ministre. En clair, une décentralisation logistique qui devrait aussi déplacer les centres de stockage vers ces zones côtières. La rançon de la croissance ? Comment expliquer cette embolie ? Pour Ousmane Gaoual Diallo, le malheur des quais congestionnés est aussi le miroir d’une économie qui pulse. L’augmentation spectaculaire de la consommation de clinker par les cimenteries locales en est l'illustration parfaite. Le ministre avance un chiffre fort : le trafic au Port autonome de Conakry aurait bondi de 47 % en l'espace de six mois. Une déferlante de marchandises que les infrastructures actuelles ont eu toutes les peines du monde à absorber. « S'il n'y avait pas de croissance importante, s'il n'y avait pas d'investissements massifs dans ce pays, on n'aurait jamais connu cette congestion », plaide-t-il. Avant de lâcher cette formule en forme de viatique : « Nous préférons avoir les conséquences de la croissance que les conséquences de l'absence de croissance et de la pauvreté. » Reste maintenant à joindre les actes à la parole sur le terrain, car les opérateurs économiques, eux, attendent des résultats.

items.title: Wontumi conviction should inspire stronger fight against galamsey – Ashigbey
items.description: Convener of the Media Coalition Against Illegal Mining, Dr Kenneth Ashigbey, says the conviction of NPP Ashanti Regional Chairman Bernard Antwi Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, should inspire more journalists, activists and citizens to intensify the fight against illegal mining. Speaking on JoyNews Today on Monday, Dr Ashigbey described the court’s decision as a significant moment in the campaign against galamsey. He expressed hope that it would encourage greater public commitment to demanding accountability from those responsible for protecting the country’s natural resources. “So we’ll be hoping that this would create the impetus for us to demand a lot more. As you said yourself, the EOCO report, we need to be able to see that. And all the people who are involved in galamsey… You know, once you are implicated in it, you need to be punished,” he said. Dr Ashigbey stressed that the verdict should not mark the end of the country’s anti-illegal mining efforts but rather serve as renewed motivation to push for stronger enforcement of the law. According to him, Ghanaians should seize the moment to demand more from political leaders, law enforcement agencies and state institutions responsible for investigating, prosecuting and sanctioning persons implicated in illegal mining. He argued that the fight against galamsey can only succeed if the law is applied consistently and without fear or favour, regardless of a suspect’s political or social standing. His comments come in the wake of the conviction of Chairman Wontumi in a high-profile illegal mining case involving Akonta Mining Limited. Wontumi and his company were prosecuted over allegations that they permitted two individuals to undertake mining activities on the company’s concession at Samreboi in the Western Region without the required ministerial approval and facilitated unlicensed mining operations. Throughout the trial, Wontumi denied all the charges and maintained that any illegal mining on the concession was carried out without his knowledge. The case, which attracted nationwide attention because of Wontumi’s position as the Ashanti Regional Chairman of the New Patriotic Party (NPP), went through months of hearings. The High Court dismissed a submission of no case by the defence, ordered Wontumi to open his defence, and later postponed judgment from July 3 to July 20 to allow his new legal team additional time to file written submissions. The court subsequently convicted Wontumi and sentenced him to 20 years’ imprisonment. Dr Ashigbey said he hopes the verdict will encourage journalists, civil society organisations and ordinary citizens to sustain pressure on authorities to pursue all illegal mining cases with the same level of commitment.

items.title: Gao : désaccord entre boulangers et revendeurs sur le prix du pain
items.description: Une incomprehension oppose les boulangers aux revendeurs de pain au sujet du prix de la miche. Les boulangers ont decide de vendre desormais le pain aux livreurs a 150 francs CFA, laissant a ces derniers la liberte de fixer leur prix de revente. Cette decision fait craindre une augmentation du prix paye par les consommateurs. Les revendeurs ont donc alerte les autorites afin qu'elles interviennent pour trouver une solution et preserver l'accessibilite du pain. L’article Gao : désaccord entre boulangers et revendeurs sur le prix du pain est apparu en premier sur Studio Tamani - Informations, débats, magazines : toute l’actualité du Mali, en 5 langues .

items.title: Structuration de l’immobilier au Burkina Faso : Jean Kiendrebéogo prend les devants de la 2AI-BF
items.description: L’Association des Agents Immobiliers du Burkina Faso (2AI-BF) a renouvelé les membres de son bureau exécutif pour un mandat de trois ans, ce vendredi 17 juillet 2026. Cet exercice a été réalisé au cours d’une Assemblée générale extraordinaire. L’Association des Agents Immobiliers du Burkina Faso (2AI-BF) a un nouveau président. Jean Kiendrebéogo, prend les rênes de la présidence pour les trois prochaines années. Il remplace à ce poste Boubakar Compaoré, qui venait d’achever un mandat de trois ans. En quittant ses fonctions, ce dernier a dressé un bilan satisfaisant, tout en saluant la qualité de la collaboration qui a permis d’atteindre de tels résultats. Dans son intervention, il a souhaité que ces acquis soient consolidés, voire améliorés. « Ce qu’il faut retenir de mon mandat, c’est l’innovation, car nous avons quand même innové à notre manière dans la gestion de notre association. Nous avons fait valoir la culture de la démocratie, la culture de la solidarité et surtout celle de la résilience. Tout n’est pas parfait, certes, mais nous avons fait un bond en avant qui peut être salué au terme de notre mandat », a déclaré le président sortant. Aussitôt installé, le nouveau président, Jean Kiendrebéogo, entend jouer la carte du rassemblement et œuvrer à susciter davantage d’adhésions. Il porte également le projet de création d’une académie destinée à renforcer la formation des agents immobiliers évoluant sur le terrain. « Vous avez choisi de faire confiance à un programme et à une vision. Vous savez que leur mise en œuvre contribuera à construire un secteur immobilier plus organisé, plus professionnel et plus respecté au Burkina Faso. Je suis convaincu que, si nous conservons le même dynamisme, le même esprit de solidarité et la même détermination que ceux dont vous avez fait preuve ces dernières semaines, nous réussirons », a-t-il affirmé. Cette passation de charges est la troisième du genre au sein de l’association. Pour rappel, celle-ci est née en 2012 sous le nom de Fédération des Agents Immobiliers du Burkina Faso (FAIBF). Faso7 L’article Structuration de l’immobilier au Burkina Faso : Jean Kiendrebéogo prend les devants de la 2AI-BF est apparu en premier sur Faso7 .

items.title: Sandbondtenga/mobilité urbaine: Des professionnels des médias en immersion sur les chantiers du PMDUVS à Kaya
items.description: Sandbondtenga/mobilité urbaine: Des professionnels des médias en immersion sur les chantiers du PMDUVS à Kaya Kaya, 16 juil. 2026 (AIB) – Des professionnels des médias ont visité, jeudi, les chantiers de voirie et d'assainissement en cours de réalisation à Kaya, dans le cadre du Projet de Mobilité et de Développement Urbain des Villes Secondaires (PMDUVS). Selon Bruno Bonzi de la Direction de la communication et des relations presses du ministère de la Construction de la Patrie, cette immersion organisée par sa structure vise à permettre aux journalistes de « toucher du doigt la réalité des réalisations en cours », de mieux comprendre les interventions du projet et de disposer d'informations fiables pour éclairer l'opinion publique. Sur les différents chantiers de COGEB international SA, de l’entreprise Bâtiment-Travaux-Maintenance (BTM) et de l'Agence de Conseil et de Maîtrise d'Ouvrage Déléguée en Bâtiment et Aménagement Urbain (ACOMOD-Burkina), les responsables ont présenté l'état d'avancement des travaux tout en relevant les difficultés rencontrées notamment les problèmes d'approvisionnement en matériaux et l'incivisme de certains usagers malgré, selon eux, la sensibilisation. « Nous faisons face au non-respect des dispositifs de sécurité par certaines personnes notamment les enfants », a indiqué Ismaël Sayaogo, directeur des travaux de l'entreprise BTM. Néanmoins il dit pouvoir être dans le délai avec un taux d'exécution de 92%. Au-delà de la voirie, le PMDUVS prévoit la réalisation d'infrastructures sociales à Kaya. Sur le chantier de salles de classe à l'école Bonheur Ville A, le chef de projet de l'ACOMOD-Burkina, Louona Allan Romaric Koulidiati, a présenté le volet éducatif du projet. « À Kaya, nous avons à réaliser, 33 salles de classe avec des bâtiments extensibles en R+1, ainsi que des espaces verts. La mairie de Kaya est également prévue pour être réhabilitée », a-t-il précisé. Le chef de mission de contrôle, Issouf Sanogo, a indiqué que les travaux de voirie et d'assainissement, évalués à environ 24 000 mètres linéaires de caniveaux et de collecteurs, affichent un taux global d'exécution de près de 90 %. « Nous demandons aux populations d'aider à empêcher les enfants de franchir les balises de sécurité pour jouer sur les chantiers. Les médias ont également un rôle important à jouer dans cette sensibilisation », a-t-il souligné. Au terme de la visite, les participants ont salué cette initiative qui leur a permis de mieux apprécier le niveau d'avancement des travaux et les enjeux des infrastructures en cours de réalisation. Mis en œuvre sur cinq ans, le PMDUVS vise à améliorer la mobilité urbaine, l'accès aux services de base et les opportunités économiques dans les villes de Bobo-Dioulasso, Kaya et Ouahigouya, tout en renforçant les capacités des collectivités territoriales. Agence d'information du Burkina AIO/bak

items.title: Osun 2026: INEC begins distribution of PVC Wednesday
items.description: By Shina Abubakar Osogbo As the August 15 governorship election approaches, the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, will commence distribution of Permanent Voter Cards, PVCs, on Wednesday across the state. A statement issued by the State Resident Electoral Commissioner, REC, Mrs Oluwatoyin Babalola, stated that the exercise is for persons who registered; applied for transfer of registration; requested replacement of lost and damaged PVCs; or updated their records during the recently concluded Continuous Voter Registration, CVR, exercise. She added that the distribution exercise will begin at the 332 registration areas across the state from Wednesday, July 22, to Tuesday, July 28, between 9 a.m. and 3 p.m. daily, including weekends. “The exercise will subsequently continue at INEC Local Government Area offices from Wednesday, July 29, to Friday, August 7, also from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. daily,” she added. Babalola reminded voters that PVC collection is strictly personal, stressing that collection by proxy is prohibited and constitutes an offence under the law. She urged all eligible voters to collect their PVCs within the stipulated period, noting that the Permanent Voter Card remains the only valid means of accreditation for voting on election day. She reaffirmed the commission’s commitment to conducting a free, fair, credible, inclusive and transparent governorship election in Osun State. The post Osun 2026: INEC begins distribution of PVC Wednesday appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Conseils et astuces pour travailler avec des personnes incompétentes
items.description: Des personnes incompétentes sont présentes dans des nombreuses organisations. Leur incompétence se manifeste par des erreurs répétées, des délais non respectées et une difficulté à s’adapter aux changements. Un incompétent peut être un collègue, un subordonné ou bien votre propre manager. Ils peuvent occuper durablement des postes de direction et impacter négativement la marche de l’organisation. C’est le phénomène appelé « Kakistocratie ».

items.title: HUDAI Foundation, Türkiye-based Tivnikli Foundation extend flood relief support to victims
items.description: The Human Development Association International (HUDAI Foundation), in partnership with the Türkiye-based Tivnikli Foundation, has provided emergency relief and financial assistance to communities affected by the devastating floods that hit parts of Ghana on June 29, 2026. The humanitarian intervention, according to the foundations, is aimed at supporting families displaced by the floods and helping victims recover from the impact of the disaster. Following reports of the flooding, the leadership of HUDAI Foundation mobilised its board of trustees to coordinate an emergency response programme to provide immediate support to affected individuals and families. As part of the first phase of the intervention, the foundations have distributed 150 relief packages containing essential food items, clean water and sanitary supplies to flood victims. The packages were accompanied by cash support to help beneficiaries meet urgent needs arising from the disaster. The second phase of the initiative involves the distribution of direct financial assistance to an additional 180 victims. The foundations say the cash donations are intended to give affected families the flexibility to purchase necessities and begin rebuilding their livelihoods. In a statement, HUDAI Foundation and Tivnikli Foundation expressed solidarity with the government and people of Ghana, describing the support as part of their commitment to standing with communities during times of crisis. “We stand shoulder to shoulder with Ghana during this trial. It is our collective prayer that Almighty Allah grants fortitude to the affected families, restores what was lost, and continues to guide, bless, and protect the entire nation,” the foundations said. The initiative is being supported by the management of the National Mosque Complex and Tinasco School, with the organisations pledging continued collaboration with local authorities to support long-term recovery efforts. The foundations say they will continue monitoring the situation and exploring further interventions aimed at strengthening community resilience in flood-affected areas.

items.title: Classement FIFA : le Maroc grimpe à la 6e place mondiale
items.description: Le Maroc occupe désormais la 6e place du classement mondial de la FIFA avec 1 803,99 points, gagnant une position à l’issue de la Coupe du monde 2026. Les Lions de l’Atlas confortent par la même occasion leur statut de première sélection africaine et arabe, tandis que l’Espagne s’installe en tête du classement après son sacre mondial. Avant le Mondial, le Maroc figurait au 7e rang mondial avec 1 755 points dans le classement publié le 11 juin dernier. Son parcours jusqu’en quarts de finale de la Coupe du monde lui a permis de récolter 48,99 points supplémentaires et de progresser d’une place dans la hiérarchie internationale. Les Lions de l’Atlas ont réalisé un parcours solide durant la compétition, rivalisant avec plusieurs grandes nations avant d’être éliminés par la France en quarts de finale. Ces résultats permettent au Royaume de confirmer son installation durable parmi les meilleures sélections de la planète. À l’échelle africaine, le Maroc conserve largement son statut de sélection la mieux classée. Il devance le Sénégal, 18e mondial, l’Égypte (24e), le Nigeria (26e), l’Algérie (29e) et la Côte d’Ivoire (31e). La sélection marocaine reste également en tête des nations arabes. Derrière elle figurent notamment l’Égypte (24e), l’Algérie (29e), la Tunisie (57e), l’Arabie saoudite (58e) et le Qatar (59e). Cette progression confirme la régularité du Maroc au plus haut niveau international. Après ses performances réalisées lors des dernières grandes compétitions, les Lions de l’Atlas consolident ainsi leur place dans le Top 10 mondial et leur leadership sur les scènes africaine et arabe. Au sommet de la hiérarchie, l’Espagne s’empare de la première place mondiale après son sacre à la Coupe du monde 2026. La Roja devance l’Argentine, désormais deuxième. La France complète le podium à la troisième place, devant l’Angleterre, quatrième, et le Brésil, cinquième. Le Maroc arrive juste derrière ces cinq puissances du football mondial et occupe donc la 6e place, confirmant son statut parmi l’élite internationale après une nouvelle campagne mondiale remarquée. The post Classement FIFA : le Maroc grimpe à la 6e place mondiale appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: IFCO 2026 to position Istanbul as global hub for fashion, textiles and apparel trade
items.description: The Istanbul Fashion Connection (IFCO) 2026 is set to bring together leading global fashion brands, manufacturers, buyers and designers when it returns to the Istanbul Expo Centre in August next year, reinforcing Türkiye’s growing role in the international apparel and textile trade. The four-day exhibition is scheduled to take place from 19 to 21 August 2026 and is expected to serve as a major B2B platform for the global fashion industry, connecting suppliers and buyers across womenswear, menswear, kidswear, denim, sportswear, footwear, accessories and lingerie. Organised by İTKİB Fairs, IFCO has developed into one of Europe’s key ready-to-wear and fashion trade fairs, providing a structured environment where brands meet collaborators, businesses connect with new partners, and designers showcase seasonal collections to international buyers. The event is expected to attract hundreds of exhibitors and tens of thousands of trade visitors from more than 120 countries, making it one of the region’s largest sourcing platforms for the apparel industry. A key feature of the fair is its strong emphasis on business-to-business (B2B) matchmaking, allowing exhibitors and buyers to pre-schedule meetings to explore partnerships, distribution agreements and wholesale opportunities. Organisers say this system is designed to streamline trade interactions and improve efficiency for global sourcing. IFCO will also showcase specialised segments, including womenswear, menswear, kidswear, denim, lingerie and fashion innovation zones, reflecting the full breadth of the international fashion supply chain. Beyond exhibitions, the fair will feature industry discussions and trend-focused sessions on sustainability, digital transformation, and evolving global fashion markets, as the sector adapts to changing consumer demand and production models. With Istanbul’s strategic position bridging Europe and Asia, IFCO 2026 is expected to further strengthen the city’s status as a central meeting point for global fashion trade and production networks.

items.title: Comment lutter contre les violences basées sur le genre ?
items.description: Les violences basées sur le genre (VBG) constituent un défi majeur en République démocratique du Congo. Elles touchent une proportion importante de femmes et de filles, notamment dans l'est du pays. Les conflits armés, l'impunité et les inégalités exacerbent ce phénomène, malgré les efforts de prise en charge et le cadre juridique. En effet, ces femmes et filles subissent des violences sexuelles et son vulnérables aux agressions et aux abus de toute sortes.

items.title: CAGG rejects ‘era of impunity is over’ claim, calls for tougher anti-galamsey
items.description: The Ghana Coalition Against Galamsey (CAGG) has challenged the government’s assertion that the “era of impunity is over” in the fight against illegal mining (galamsey), insisting that persistent lawlessness across several mining hotspots demonstrates that the battle against galamsey is far from won. In a press statement issued in Accra on Monday, July 20, the coalition acknowledged what it described as genuine progress made by the Ministry of Lands and Natural Resources but maintained that enforcement remained inconsistent and insufficient to protect the country’s forests, rivers and mining communities. It argued that effective environmental protection required sustained and impartial law enforcement rather than periodic public briefings. “Ghana’s forests, rivers and mining communities are not protected by press briefings. They are protected by sustained, fearless and uniform enforcement, applied equally in every region, against every excavator, and irrespective of whose interests stand in the way,” the statement said. Achimota Forest decision welcomed The coalition commended the government for revoking Executive Instrument (E.I.) 144, as amended by E.I. 234, which had degazetted portions of the Achimota Forest Reserve, describing the Cabinet decision as a bold step towards safeguarding one of Accra’s key ecological assets. It also praised the Attorney-General for withdrawing charges against the “Atronsu Five” and urged the Attorney-General and the Inspector-General of Police to ensure justice in the murder of 13-year-old Pious Nketia Nkansah. Forests still under threat Despite acknowledging efforts to clear illegal miners from forest reserve “red zones”, the coalition said Ghana’s forests remain under severe pressure from illegal mining. It cited the Atewa Range Forest Reserve, Cape Three Points, Afao Hills Forest Reserve and the Draw River Forest Reserve as areas still facing significant threats. CAGG called for increased resources for the Forestry Commission, the establishment of protected camps in forest reserves and renewed efforts to fulfil the government’s pledge to convert the Atewa Forest into a national park. ‘Evidence of impunity remains’ The coalition strongly disagreed with the Ministry’s conclusion that impunity had ended, pointing to continuing illegal mining activities in parts of the Eastern, Ashanti, Bono and Western regions. It cited ongoing illegal mining around the Birim, Densu and Ayensu river basins, activities near the Bui Hydroelectric Dam, mining around Lake Bosomtwe and operations in the Ahwiren wetlands as evidence that illegal mining continues largely unchecked. The coalition questioned what it described as perceived selective enforcement in some regions and called on President John Dramani Mahama to ensure those implicated in illegal mining offences are investigated and prosecuted without fear or favour. Concerns over NAIMOS CAGG also raised concerns about the operations of the National Anti-Illegal Mining Operations Secretariat (NAIMOS). While acknowledging the secretariat’s record of more than 200 operations, the coalition said allegations of selective enforcement and reports that seized equipment may have been returned to offenders after payments required urgent investigation. It urged NAIMOS to publicly address the allegations and improve accountability by publishing details of its complaints mechanism and actions taken on public reports. Police, MMDAs urged to intensify enforcement The coalition further criticised what it described as weak enforcement by sections of the Ghana Police Service, arguing that deploying more Blue Water Guards alone would not curb illegal mining unless reports received from the field resulted in swift enforcement action. It called for stricter supervision of police commanders in mining areas, stronger action against underperforming officers and tighter controls on the movement of excavators and the sale of fuel to illegal mining operators. Call for broader reforms CAGG welcomed government’s plans to amend the Minerals and Mining Act, 2006 (Act 703), but stressed that any revised law must include stronger safeguards for forests, water bodies and mining communities. It also called for the publication of the Constitutional Review Committee’s report, the prosecution of alleged illegal mining kingpins, greater transparency in environmental governance and regular publication of water quality, forest status and prosecution reports. The coalition said it remained ready to work with government to strengthen environmental governance but warned that failure to decisively tackle illegal mining would have long-term consequences. “We either deal with this menace or we perish, because without clean water, clean air and standing forests, we are done as a people.” Final Press Statement CAGG July 2026 – The Era of _260720_104259 Download

items.title: 5 things to know about Andy Burnham, the UK’s new prime minister
items.description: Andy Burnham officially became the United Kingdom’s new prime minister on Monday after succeeding Keir Starmer as leader of the governing Labour Party, making him the seventh British prime minister in a decade. The former Greater Manchester mayor took office after Starmer resigned and formally handed over power following an audience with King Charles III at Buckingham Palace. Here are five key things to know about Britain’s new leader: 1. He is a veteran Labour politician Andy Burnham, 56, has spent more than two decades in frontline British politics. Before becoming prime minister, he served as the Mayor of Greater Manchester, a role he held since 2017. Earlier in his career, Burnham was a Labour MP and held several cabinet positions under former Prime Ministers Tony Blair and Gordon Brown, including serving as Health Secretary, Culture Secretary and Chief Secretary to the Treasury. 2. He earned the nickname “King of the North” Burnham became one of Britain’s most recognisable regional politicians during his time as mayor. His outspoken defence of Greater Manchester during the COVID-19 pandemic and his campaign for greater powers and funding for England’s regions earned him the nickname “King of the North.” Supporters credit him with improving public transport in Greater Manchester and championing greater devolution away from Westminster. 3. He replaces Keir Starmer without a general election Burnham’s arrival in Downing Street did not require a nationwide vote. Under the UK’s parliamentary system, the governing party can replace its leader during a parliamentary term. Because Labour still commands a majority in Parliament, Burnham automatically became prime minister after winning the party leadership following Starmer’s resignation. The next UK general election is not due until 2029 unless Burnham decides to call one earlier. 4. He has promised to “rewire Britain” In his first address as prime minister, Burnham pledged to “rewire Britain” and restore trust in politics. His priorities include tackling the cost-of-living crisis, improving struggling public services, reducing regional inequalities and creating what he describes as a more stable and responsible style of government. He has also signalled plans to decentralise power from Westminster and give more authority to local communities. 5. He faces major challenges from day one Burnham inherits a difficult political and economic landscape. Among the biggest issues awaiting the new prime minister are Britain’s high cost of living, pressure on the National Health Service (NHS), sluggish economic growth, housing shortages and concerns over public finances. He will also have to unite the Labour Party after Starmer’s departure while preparing the government for the next general election. Vanguard News The post 5 things to know about Andy Burnham, the UK’s new prime minister appeared first on Vanguard News .

Monitoring des médias à l’ère du numérique : la HAC réussit un maillage territorial inédit !
items.description: Sous le leadership de son président, Boubacar Yacine Diallo, la Haute Autorité de la Communication (HAC) vient de réussir un pari inédit : l'institution compte désormais un superviseur dans chaque préfecture de la Guinée. Il y a quelques jours, par décision du président Boubacar Yacine Diallo, des superviseurs préfectoraux et communaux ont fait leur apparition dans le dispositif de la HAC en Guinée. Ces professionnels, reconnus dans le secteur de l'information et de la communication, ont été désignés non seulement pour renforcer la régulation des médias locaux, mais aussi pour servir de relais entre l'institution de régulation et les acteurs des médias. Durant la campagne médiatique des élections législatives et communales du 31 mai 2026 , ces superviseurs locaux ont fait leur baptême du feu. Avec engagement et sens des responsabilités, ils ont assuré le monitoring des médias dans les préfectures où la présence des points focaux régionaux de la HAC pouvait être difficile. Avec assiduité, ils ont effectué un suivi régulier des médias de leurs circonscriptions électorales, signalé les manquements, conseillé les professionnels des médias et transmis des rapports quotidiens à la HAC. Leur vigilance s'est également étendue aux acteurs locaux des réseaux sociaux et des autres plateformes numériques dont les activités pouvaient avoir un impact direct sur les électeurs. Afin de mieux réussir l'organisation des élections couplées législatives et communales — une première en République de Guinée —, la Haute Autorité de la Communication, sous Boubacar Yacine Diallo, a fait preuve d'une vision stratégique et d'une ingénierie organisationnelle qui marqueront durablement l'histoire de la régulation des médias, tous supports confondus, en Guinée et dans la sous-région. En temps réel, durant cette campagne électorale de proximité, le réseau de superviseurs et de coordonnateurs (commissaires de la HAC), déployés sur toute l'étendue du territoire national, a permis de transmettre à la Coordination nationale, basée à Conakry, 92 rapports quotidiens offrant une photographie fidèle de la campagne médiatique dans chacune des circonscriptions électorales. Pendant toute la durée de la campagne, soit 28 jours, plus de 2 576 rapports ont été reçus par le président de la HAC et les m embres de la Coordination nationale. La réussite de la campagne médiatique des dernières élections de proximité, saluée par l'opinion nationale ainsi que par les institutions de la République et les plus hautes autorités du pays, trouve son origine dans cette organisation. Cette réussite est également présentée comme le reflet de la vision et de la compétence d ’ un homme : Boubacar Yacine Diallo. Une fondation solide pour l'avenir La Constitution de la République de Guinée a élargi les prérogatives de la future institution de régulation de la communication. Celles-ci s'étendent désormais à la prise en compte des réseaux sociaux et des autres plateformes numériques de communication. Le dispositif déjà initié et mis en place par la HAC constitue ainsi une solide fondation . Il contribuera, sur l'ensemble du territoire national, à détecter, identifier et traiter les dérives en matière de communication. Dans les régions administratives, ces superviseurs préfectoraux évolueront sous l'autorité des points focaux régionaux, installés dans les chefs-lieux. Périodiquement, ils devront adresser leurs rapports d'activités à ces représentants régionaux, expérimentés dans le domaine du monitoring des médias. Faut-il rappeler que, dans les régions de Boké, Kindia, Mamou, Labé, Faranah, N'Zérékoré et Kankan, le président de la République, Son Excellence Mamadi Doumbouya, a soutenu la construction des sièges régionaux de la HAC ? Ces infrastructures, connectées au centre national de monitoring de Conakry, dessinent l'avenir de la régulation des médias en Guinée. À tout seigneur, tout honneur, dit-on. Boubacar Yacine Diallo est, sans doute, un génie de la communication qui met son expérience et son expertise au service de la Nation. Contrairement aux apôtres de la diffamat ion qui pensent être des parangons de v ertus. Ils se présentent comme les seuls défenseurs de la liberté de la presse, mais ignorent parfois les textes qui régissent le secteur . Ils ne peuvent pas savoir que les décisions prises par la HAC ne relèvent pas de la volonté d'un individu, mais de l'application des lois de la République. Lorsque la HAC interpelle un journaliste ou demande des explications à un média qui s'écarte des règles déontologiques, il ne s'agit pas de museler la presse. Il s'agit, au contraire, de protéger la profession contre les pratiques qui portent atteinte à sa crédibilité sous couvert d'investigation. Finalement, avec les détracteurs de la HAC, tout semble simple : tant que la HAC ferme les yeux sur les dérives, elle est jugée tolérante ; dès qu'elle applique la loi, elle est qualifiée de liberticide. Heureusement, face aux tempêtes de l ’ indignation sélective et des diatribes de caniveau, Boubacar Yacine DIALLO et se s collaborateurs restent fidèles à eux-mêmes : dro its , s ereins et imperturbables. Jacques Traoré L’article Monitoring des médias à l’ère du numérique : la HAC réussit un maillage territorial inédit ! est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .

items.title: En Foios, el pueblo de Ferran Torres: “Es un ‘bon xiquet’, el mejor del mundo y el más guapo”
items.description: “Es muy buen chico y su familia, también”, dice una vecina, apostada en la acera donde el sol no cae a plomo. “Dejen pasar, que están molestando”, pide otra mujer sonriendo mientras empuja un carro de la compra. Al enterarse de que la comidilla de la charla es Ferran Torres se une al coro: “Sí, sí, siempre ha sido un bon xiquet, el mejor del mundo y el más guapo”. “Bueno, después de mi hijo”, apostilla una tercera en el centro de Foios. El nombre del pueblo se ha catapultado por el gol marcado por su vecino más célebre en Nueva Jersey, a más de 6.000 kilómetros. Seguir leyendo

items.title: Fuel tanker explosion near Kpone Barrier causes traffic disruption on Tema-Aflao Road
items.description: A fuel tanker has burst into flames near Kpone Barrier on the Tema-Aflao Road, leaving the driver and his mate feared trapped inside the vehicle. The tanker, with registration number GT 8261-23 , reportedly overturned after a front tyre burst before erupting into a massive fire moments later. The incident has brought traffic on the busy stretch to a standstill, with hundreds of commuters stranded as emergency responders work to manage the situation. Eyewitnesses said the fire spread rapidly after the tanker overturned, raising concerns about the safety of nearby facilities. Personnel from the Ghana National Fire Service (GNFS) are currently at the scene battling the blaze and working to prevent it from spreading to the nearby Bulk Oil Storage and Transportation Company Limited (BOST) fuel storage facility, located about 200 metres away. The cause of the explosion has not yet been officially confirmed. Authorities are expected to provide further details on possible casualties, the extent of damage and efforts to restore traffic flow.

items.title: Hamas says Khalil al-Hayya elected as group's new chief
items.description: Hamas announced Monday that it had elected Khalil al-Hayya , the group’s lead negotiator in indirect ceasefire talks with Israel, as the new head of its political bureau. “The Islamic Resistance Movement (Hamas) announces the election of brother mujahid Khalil al-Hayya as head of the movement’s political bureau, succeeding the martyred leader Yahya al-Sinwar ,” Hamas said in a statement. Sinwar, whom Israel accused of being one of the principal architects of the October 7, 2023, attacks, was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza a year later. Since his death, Hamas has been led by a five-member leadership council headed by long-time senior official Mohammed Darwish. Born in 1960, Hayya survived an Israeli assassination attempt in Doha last year, though one of his sons was killed in the strike. Hayya defeated former Hamas chief and veteran leader Khaled Meshaal in the leadership contest. “Khalil al-Hayya was elected by an overwhelming majority,” a senior Hamas official told AFP , adding that he is expected to formally assume the role next week. “The political bureau will meet in Istanbul within days to form committees and begin restructuring Hamas’s leadership bodies.” The official said the current five-member leadership council will be dissolved, with its members submitting their resignations to the group’s Shura Council. “The election process was conducted through a highly complex and secretive mechanism because of the difficult security situation and the occupation’s assassination of several political and military leaders,” a source in Hamas said. Representatives of Hamas’s armed wing, the Ezzedine Al-Qassam Brigades, also declared their “full support” to Hayya. Hailing from the Gaza Strip, Hayya has lost several close relatives, including his eldest son, to Israeli strikes in the Gaza Strip, and is a veteran member of the group. Regarded as having good ties with Iran, he has been closely involved in the group’s efforts to broker several truces with Israel, playing a key role in ending a 2014 conflict and again in attempts to secure an end to the current Gaza conflict. Hayya has been part of Hamas since it was set up in 1987. In the early 1980s, he joined the Muslim Brotherhood, the movement from which Hamas emerged, sources say. In Gaza, he was detained several times by Israel. In 2007, an Israeli air strike hit his family home in Gaza City’s Sejaiyeh quarter, killing several of his relatives, and during the 2014 fighting between Hamas and Israel, the house of Hayya’s eldest son, Osama, was bombed, killing him, his wife and three of their children. Hayya was not there during the attacks. He left Gaza several years ago, serving as a Hamas point person for ties with the Arab and Islamic worlds and basing himself in Qatar for the role.

items.title: Supreme Court dismisses APSL bid to revive US$33.3m Boankra arbitration award
items.description: The Supreme Court of Ghana has unanimously dismissed an application by Ashanti Port Services Limited (APSL) that sought to overturn a High Court decision nullifying a US$33.3 million arbitral award originally granted to APSL against Justmoh Construction Limited. The decision delivered by a five-member panel chaired by Chief Justice Paul Baffoe-Bonnie, and including Justices Asiedu, Richard Adjei-Frimpong Kwofie, Senyo Dzamefe, and Kodwo Amaleboba, ruled that APSL’s invocation of the court’s supervisory jurisdiction completely lacked merit. The ruling seeks to end a prolonged legal battle over the Boankra Inland Logistics Terminal Project. Both the High Court and the Supreme Court agreed that APSL lacked the legal and corporate capacity to initiate arbitration when the proceedings began. The courts also found that attempts by APSL to retroactively approve the arbitration during a later board meeting constituted a fundamental defect that could not cure the initial lack of authority. Lawyers for Justmoh Construction have welcomed the decision. Lead Counsel, Professor Kwame Gyan said the outcome was expected given the strength of the facts and the law. “We have always had confidence in our brief because we know the facts support our case. It comes as no surprise that the Supreme Court agrees with us. Due process has won,” he said. The Boankra Inland Logistics Terminal Project is a flagship state infrastructure initiative designed to boost Ghana’s transit trade and logistics. The project involves key stakeholders including the Ministry of Transport, the Ghana Shippers’ Authority, and the Ghana Ports and Harbours Authority, GPHA. In August 2022, APSL appointed Justmoh Construction as the main contractor for Phase 1A of the project. APSL however failed to secure the necessary financing required under its concession agreement. GPHA subsequently stepped in and paid US$33.3 million under a share subscription arrangement, with the funds transferred directly to Justmoh as mobilisation capital to start work. In August 2023, the Ghana Shippers’ Authority terminated the concession agreement due to APSL’s funding failures, leading to a state takeover. In December 2023, APSL initiated arbitration demanding that Justmoh refund the US$33.3 million to them. An arbitral tribunal initially ruled in APSL’s favor in late 2025. In setting aside the tribunal’s award, the High Court identified critical procedural and legal flaws in APSL’s actions. The court found that APSL failed to obtain valid board approval before launching arbitration, and that a subsequent January 2024 board meeting meant to ratify the lawsuit was invalid because the board lacked required representatives from crucial stakeholders like GPHA and the Ghana Shippers’ Authority. The court also held that ordering a refund to APSL would constitute unjust enrichment, because the US$33.3 million mobilisation fund was actually provided by GPHA and not advanced by APSL, meaning APSL had no legal right to claim a refund for money it never spent.

items.title: Indian police fire tear gas, baton-charge Cockroach Janta Party protesters trying to march on parliament
items.description: Indian police fired tear gas and baton-charged protesters in New Delhi on Monday as they tried to march on parliament to demand the resignation of the education minister over irregularities in major examinations. The protesters, mostly young students, ran for cover as police charged at them and fired tear gas shells near Jantar Mantar in the Indian capital. They are seeking the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over exam irregularities, including question paper leaks and technical glitches. Thousands of protesters started gathering from early Monday, defying a steady monsoon drizzle and police barricades, marking one of the biggest demonstrations in the capital in five years. “Our country deserves a government that is bothered about what the young people have to say and is willing to correct its mistakes,” said Sumit Kumar, 18. “The future belongs to us and not these politicians.” The protest comes after prominent activist Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly taken to hospital on Saturday, bringing an abrupt end to his 20-day hunger strike in support of youths and the satirical Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) movement. The CJP has won millions of followers on social media since its launch in May, tapping into widespread anger over the country’s education system and unemployment. “Is this a war zone? Why is the police resorting to tear gas assault on peaceful protesters?” CJP said in a post on X. “First, your government ignored this protest for a month, and now, when we are peacefully marching, your government is attacking us. Why?” The movement is seen as one of the biggest challenges to Prime Minister Narendra Modi since he won his third term in office in 2024. ‘We shall win’ Despite rapid economic growth, millions of people in the world’s most populous nation still struggle to find stable and well-paying jobs, fuelling discontent. Some 2.2 million aspiring medical students sat for a re-examination under tight security last month after the previous test was scrapped following a question paper leak that triggered widespread outrage. That came on top of another scandal related to the online marking system of tests taken by nearly 2m high school students. The CJP said two of its leaders met a senior minister on Monday and submitted a letter spelling out their demands after the government reached out to them in the morning. The internet was cut in parts of central New Delhi on Monday, with police saying they had not given their permission for the protest. “Any person violating these prohibitory orders shall be liable for prosecution,” Delhi Police said in a statement. Shubhi Rao, 16, said she had joined the protest to support Wangchuk and demand accountability from the government. “Prime Minister Narendra Modi has shown himself as someone who isn’t bothered about the youth of this country,” she told AFP . Modi, in a public address before the start of the monsoon session of parliament, did not mention the protesters. But Mallikarjun Kharge, a senior leader of the opposition Congress party, brought up the issue in the upper house. “Thousands of students have gathered at Jantar Mantar. The government is trying to use force, suppress voices, and drive them away,” he said. Slogans were also shouted inside the lower house by the opposition benches, demanding the resignation of the education minister and justice for students. Activists set terms to end his fast CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke and his supporters began a sit-in last month seeking the resignation of Pradhan. Wangchuk joined them on June 28, launching an indefinite hunger strike at the site. In a handwritten note from the hospital posted on his X account on Monday, Wangchuk said he would end his fast if the government took responsibility for recent failures in the education system and for paper leaks, or if CJP protesters were allowed to reach parliament and lawmakers assured them they would take up the issues. He said he would also end the hunger strike if lawmakers and leaders of various parties met him in the hospital and gave him the same assurances, since his health did not allow him to march on parliament. The rise of the CJP reflects frustrations among young Indians over issues like high youth unemployment and frequent examination paper ​leaks, analysts say. Unemployment in 2025 stood at 3.1 per cent among those aged 15 and above, government data shows, but was 9.9pc for those from 15 to 29. In urban areas, it was 13.6pc, and 8.3pc in rural regions.

items.title: 12.2m sanitary pads distributed nationwide – Haruna Iddrisu
items.description: The Minister for Education, Haruna Iddrisu, has announced that 12.2 million sanitary pads have been distributed to female students across the country, benefiting 2.5 million girls as part of government’s efforts to address menstrual hygiene challenges and improve school retention. Speaking at the Government’s Accountability Series on Monday, July 20, the Education Minister said the initiative was introduced in response to evidence that some girls were missing school or dropping out due to difficulties in accessing sanitary products. Mr Iddrisu said although the introduction of the sanitary pad support programme by Vice President Professor Jane Naana Opoku-Agyemang was initially met with mixed reactions, the impact of the intervention had demonstrated the importance of addressing menstrual hygiene needs among schoolgirls. “When Her Excellency the Vice President, Jane Naana Opoku-Agyemang, first introduced this matter of sanitary pads, there were many who didn’t appreciate it,” he said. According to Mr Iddrisu, the government’s intervention has so far resulted in the distribution of 12.2 million sanitary pads across the country, benefiting 2.5 million female students. “We have distributed 12.2 million sanitary pads across the country, benefiting 2.5 million female students across the country,” he stated. He explained that the programme forms part of broader measures aimed at removing barriers that prevent girls from fully participating in education and ensuring their continued stay in school. The Education Minister said the government would provide further details on the regional distribution of the sanitary pads when required by the media. Responding to possible requests for further details on the distribution, the education minister said the regional breakdown would be provided when needed. “I will provide the regional breakdown as and when the media needs more answers to it,” he said. Mr Iddrisu also disclosed that preparations were underway to procure additional sanitary pads for the current year to sustain the programme and ensure more girls continue to benefit. “As I speak to it, we are in the process of acquiring for this year the same quantity, or even more, of the sanitary pads for distribution to our young girls, in order that we will sustain and ensure their retention in school,” he said. The Minister said the continued provision of sanitary pads was part of government’s commitment to promoting equitable access to education and reducing challenges that affect the participation and retention of girls in schools.

items.title: Gunmen kill two women in Plateau community as residents protest over insecurity
items.description: By Golok Nanmwa JOS — Tension has gripped Gero community in Jos South Local Government Area of Plateau State following the killing of two women by suspected gunmen, an incident that triggered protests by residents on Monday. The victims were reportedly discovered dead in the early hours of the day in Gero community, located behind the Jos South Local Government Secretariat. The circumstances surrounding the killings remained unclear at the time of filing this report. The discovery sparked outrage among residents, particularly women and youths, who marched to the local government secretariat carrying the bodies of the deceased to protest the incident and demand urgent action from authorities. The protesters later blocked parts of the road leading to the secretariat, causing temporary disruption to vehicular movement as they called for improved security measures and justice for the victims. One of the protesters, Mrs. Mary Chollom, condemned the killings, describing the incident as another painful reflection of the security challenges facing communities across Plateau State. She lamented that innocent residents, including women, children and farmers, have continued to suffer the consequences of violent attacks, leaving families traumatised and communities in fear. “We are tired of burying our loved ones. These women did nothing to deserve this. Government and security agencies must take decisive action to stop these senseless killings and ensure those responsible are brought to justice,” she said. Chollom appealed to both the federal and state governments to strengthen security operations in vulnerable communities and ensure residents can go about their daily activities without fear. She also urged security agencies to intensify surveillance, identify those behind the attack and bring them to justice, noting that accountability would help restore confidence among residents. Following the protest, security personnel were deployed to the Jos South Local Government Secretariat and surrounding areas, while calm gradually returned to the community. Efforts to get official confirmation from the spokesperson of the Plateau State Police Command, DSP Alfred Alabo, were unsuccessful as of the time of filing this report. The post Gunmen kill two women in Plateau community as residents protest over insecurity appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: UNIPORT dissolves Students’ Union; NANS condemns action
items.description: The management of the University of Port Harcourt (UNIPORT) has dissolved all Students’ Union Government (SUG) activities in the institution with immediate effect. The university also directed all elected student leaders to relinquish union property and prepare to appear before an investigative panel to account for their stewardship. The directive was contained in a memorandum signed by the Dean of Student Affairs, Prof. Geoffrey Woke, on the instruction of the newly appointed Vice-Chancellor, Pro. Princewill Chike. The memorandum, dated July 19, announced the dissolution of the three arms of the Student’s Union, Hall Representatives and all other union-related activities. “I am directed by the Vice-Chancellor, Prof. Princewill Chike, to notify the three arms of the Student’s Union that they have been dissolved with immediate effect until further notice further notice,” the memo stated. The university directed all executive members of the union to hand over union property to the Office of the Dean of Student Affairs. It also ordered the surrender of official documents, financial records, keys, identification materials and other items belonging to the union. According to the memo, affected student leaders are expected to appear before an investigative panel whenever invited. The university warned that failure to comply with the directive would attract disciplinary sanctions. The News Agency of Nigeria (NAN) reports that the institution did not disclose the reasons for the dissolution or the specific issues under investigation. Reacting, the National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) condemned the action, describing it as a violation of student’s democratic rights. The National President of NANS, Mr Akinteye Afeez, made the association’s position known in a statement issued on Monday. Afeez said that while university authorities had the right to investigate allegations against student leaders, due process must be followed. “Dissolving a democratically elected union without due process, transparency and fair hearing amounts to an abuse of administrative powers,” he said. According to him, the Student’s Union remains the legitimate representative body of students and cannot be dissolved through an administrative directive without clear justification. He argued that affected officials should first be informed of the allegations against them and be given an opportunity to defend themselves. Afeez also raised concerns over the directive requiring union leaders to surrender documents and financial records before appearing before the investigative panel. “This sequence of events raises questions about the transparency, fairness and independence of the investigative process,” he stated. The NANS president called on the university management to suspend implementation of the dissolution order and publicly disclose the reasons behind the action. He demanded the constitution of an impartial investigative panel and the guarantee of fair hearing for all affected student leaders. “The university authorities must refrain from intimidation, victimisation or disciplinary action against student leaders for carrying out their representative responsibilities,” he said. Afeez stressed that NANS was not opposed to accountability and supported sanctions against any student leader found culpable of misconduct after due process. He, however, warned that accountability should not be used as a pretext to weaken democratic institutions or suppress legitimate student representation on campus. “We urged UNIPORT authorities to resolve disagreements through dialogue, transparent investigation and adherence to established procedures,” he added. He disclosed that the association had commenced consultations with relevant stakeholders and would pursue lawful means to protect students’ rights and democratic institutions. Afeez reiterated NANS’ demand for the immediate reversal of the dissolution order pending the outcome of what he described as a transparent, impartial and credible investigation. (NAN) The post UNIPORT dissolves Students’ Union; NANS condemns action appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Mahama approves TVET Fund to boost technical education – Haruna Iddrisu
items.description: Minister for Education, Haruna Iddrisu, has announced that President John Mahama and Cabinet have given approval in principle for the establishment of a dedicated Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Fund to strengthen skills development across the country. Speaking at the Government’s Accountability Series on Monday, July 20, the Minister said the fund is expected to become operational in January 2027 after the government completes work on its funding arrangements. “I’m happy to note that President Mahama and his Cabinet have given in-principle approval for a TVET Fund to be established,” he said. “So, from January 2027, Ghana will have a dedicated TVET Fund.” The Minister disclosed that President Mahama has tasked the Ministers for Finance, Education, and Energy to develop a sustainable funding model for the initiative. “The President has tasked three ministers, the Minister for Finance, the Minister for Education, and the Minister for Energy, to work out the modalities of how it will be funded,” he said. “We are in discussion, and we will share with the public what it is that we are able to do.” Mr Iddrisu said the fund would help improve practical skills training by financing the construction of workshops and providing modern equipment for technical institutions. He said that Ghana’s TVET system currently places too much emphasis on classroom teaching instead of practical learning. “We want to emphasise more on training and work-based learning and depart from the current system where you still do 90 per cent theory and 10 per cent practical,” he said. “We intend that when we establish the TVET Fund, it will support you to build more workshops and equip them adequately.” The Minister added that work is already underway to establish TVET Centres of Excellence in Salaga and Kumasi to promote innovation and expose students to real workplace environments. “I’m aware that work is ongoing for a TVET Centre of Excellence in Salaga and in Kumasi to promote innovation and stimulate a real work environment,” he said. Mr Iddrisu also announced plans to expand access to secondary education through the construction of more community day senior high schools. He said despite the significant increase in enrolment under the Free Senior High School policy, many Members of Parliament continue to request the conversion of day schools into boarding schools. “It is the view of President Mahama that we should encourage the establishment of community day-care schools. In the category of what is in Kwabenya. So we’ll build one of those in Nima and one in Kasoa as part of the STAR-J project,” he said. He disclosed that new community day schools are planned for Nkwanta, Amasaman, and Kasoa under the government’s STAR-J project. The Minister further revealed that the Volta Region will benefit from a new special needs education facility under the same project to support learners with disabilities. “I’m also happy to note that under the STAR-J project, the Volta Region will benefit from a new special needs education facility to support learners with disability in that area,” he said. “The second location for the second special needs school is now being debated with data between Kintampo and another town in the Upper East Region,” he added.

items.title: BoG signals end of disinflation, puts inflation risks under spotlight
items.description: The Governor of the Bank of Ghana (BoG), Dr. Johnson Asiama, says the country’s prolonged period of disinflation has come to an end, with the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) now assessing whether the recent rise in inflation is temporary or signals a more persistent shift in the inflation outlook. Speaking at the opening of the 131st MPC meeting in Accra, Dr. Asiama said headline inflation has risen for three consecutive months, increasing from 3.2 percent in March to 5.3 percent in June, largely driven by higher transport and haulage costs. Despite the increase, he noted that inflation remains below the Bank of Ghana’s target band of 8 percent, plus or minus two percentage points, and well below the 13.7 percent recorded during the same period last year. “Headline inflation has risen for three consecutive months from 3.2 percent in March to 5.3 percent in June, driven largely by transport and haulage prices.” Dr. Asiama said the committee’s key task this week is to determine whether the recent increase in inflation represents a normal return towards the central bank’s target range or the start of a more sustained inflationary trend. “The prolonged disinflation phase has ended indeed, and inflation is now returning towards the target band. Whether that return reflects orderly normalisation or the beginning of a more persistent change in the outlook is a central question for our meeting this week.” He added that the MPC would also assess the impact of external commodity price pressures, as well as potential increases in utility tariffs and transport fares, on the country’s inflation outlook. “The central judgement in this is not whether inflation has moved but whether it is beginning to influence the expectations that shape price behaviour.” The Monetary Policy Committee is expected to announce its latest interest rate decision at the conclusion of its meetings later this week, with markets closely watching whether policymakers maintain the current policy stance or signal a shift in response to evolving inflationary pressures.

items.title: Le HCP anticipe un allègement du déficit commercial à 21,1% du PIB en 2027
items.description: Le déficit commercial devrait marquer un creusement en 2026, passant de 20,5% du produit intérieur brut (PIB) à 21,9%, avant de s'alléger à 21,1% en 2027, selon le Haut-Commissariat au Plan (HCP). « Les échanges extérieurs en 2026 devraient évoluer dans un contexte marqué par des facteurs d'offre favorables et des conditions externes contraignantes (…) Les exportations seraient ainsi pénalisées par une demande adressée au Maroc en ralentissement, sous l'effet du ralentissement de l'activité économique chez les principaux partenaires » , indique le HCP dans son Budget Économique Exploratoire 2027, précisant que le volume des exportations de biens se stabiliserait autour de 4% en 2026 avant de marquer une reprise à 5,7% en 2027. Le volume des importations de biens devrait, quant à lui, enregistrer une hausse de 8,5% en 2026, et de 7,3% en 2027, d'après la même source. Sur le plan des services, segment relativement moins affecté par les perturbations des chaînes de valeur mondiales, les indicateurs témoignent de la poursuite de la bonne tenue des recettes touristiques et du transport. Dans ces conditions, la facture des importations devrait s'alourdir, induisant une progression de la valeur des importations des biens et services de 10,5% en 2026, avant une décélération à 6,1% en 2027. Par ailleurs, la valeur des exportations des biens et services augmenterait de 8,3% en 2026 et de 7,5% en 2027. Par ailleurs, le HCP prévoit que l'excédent de la balance des services, faisant preuve d'une solidité structurelle, permettrait de ramener le déficit en ressources à seulement 12,3% du PIB en 2026 et à 10,9% en 2027. S'agissant des transferts des Marocains résidant à l'étranger (MRE), ils devraient maintenir leur tendance favorable malgré la dégradation du pouvoir d'achat en Europe. Ainsi, le déficit du compte courant atteindrait 3,9 % du PIB en 2026, contre 2,4% en 2025, avant de s'alléger légèrement à 3,6% du PIB en 2027. Le Budget économique exploratoire du HCP est de nature à permettre aux décideurs d'appréhender l'évolution économique prévue en 2026 et 2027. Il sert ainsi de référence pour fixer les objectifs macroéconomiques et les éventuelles mesures à mettre en œuvre, en vue de la préparation de la Loi de Finances 2027. L'élaboration de ce document tient compte des agrégats provisoires arrêtés par la comptabilité nationale ainsi que des résultats des enquêtes trimestrielles et des travaux de suivi et d'analyse de la conjoncture menés par le HCP, et par les différentes institutions nationales et internationales. The post Le HCP anticipe un allègement du déficit commercial à 21,1% du PIB en 2027 appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Man charged over incident where car rams into crowd
items.description: A 29-year-old man has been charged with dangerous driving after a car was driven into a crowd in County Dublin on Saturday. Footage of the incident, on Harbour Road, Skerries, has been widely shared on social media and appears to show people fighting in the area and a car being driven at a group of people. Three teenagers, two girls and a boy, were taken to hospital with non-life threatening injuries after the incident, Irish broadcaster RTÉ has reported. Sean McGlone, from Beechwood Drive in Drogheda, appeared before Balbriggan District Court on Monday. The court heard that McGlone was arrested in his home at about 18:00 local time on Sunday on suspicion of the driving offence of endangerment. He was then taken to the Garda (Irish police) station where he was charged. An application for bail was made and the officer did not object, so long as a number of conditions were imposed. Judge Stephanie Coggins granted bail on his own bond of €5,000 with a lodgement of €1,000 on Monday. McGlone, who drives a van as part of his job, must sign on three times a week and avoid the Balbriggan and Skerries area. He must not have any contact with witnesses, should not consume alcohol and he should be of good behaviour.

items.title: Here’s the latest.
Haruna Iddrisu announces revised basic school curriculum with artificial intelligence, coding and TVET
items.description: Minister for Education, Haruna Iddrisu, has announced that Ghana’s revised basic school curriculum, which introduces coding, artificial intelligence (AI), electronics and technical and vocational education and training (TVET), will be presented to the Ministry of Education this week. Speaking at the Government’s Accountability Series on Monday, July 20, the Minister said the revised curriculum, covering kindergarten to junior high school (JHS), is one of the government’s major education reforms and is aimed at equipping learners with skills needed for the future. According to him, the National Council for Curriculum and Assessment (NaCCA) was tasked with reviewing the curriculum to better prepare pupils for a technology-driven world. “One of the most significant decisions we took as government was to revise our education curriculum, and the agency responsible is NaCCA. They were asked to revise our education curriculum from kindergarten to junior high school,” he said. The Minister disclosed that the revised curriculum would be formally presented to the Ministry of Education on Wednesday before being submitted to President John Mahama for approval and subsequently to Cabinet and Parliament for consideration. “I’m happy to note that on Wednesday, a revised curriculum from kindergarten to junior high school will be presented formally to the Ministry of Education for onward submission to the President and for the policy to go to Cabinet and Parliament,” he stated. He said the review is intended to strengthen science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) education through the introduction of new learning areas. “The revision of our curriculum is to ensure a Smart Start curriculum for greater integration of technology, science, engineering, and mathematics. We have promised to introduce coding, electronics, artificial intelligence, and TVET into the basic education curriculum. And this will be the new addition,” he said. Beyond technology-focused subjects, the Minister said the revised curriculum would place greater emphasis on ethics, patriotism and civic responsibility to help shape responsible citizens. “More importantly, ethics, ethical values, how the child grows up, knowing what the state resources are, knowing how to be patriotic and responsible, and knowing how to be civically responsible will be an integral part of our revised curriculum,” Mr Iddrisu said. The Minister also highlighted efforts to strengthen basic education through increased funding, saying the government has improved the capitation grant and is ensuring its timely release to schools. He added that payments have already been made to beneficiaries, with details expected to be provided as part of the Ministry’s presentation. “At the basic education level, we have done some spending, improving the capitation grant and ensuring that it’s released in a timely. We have made some payments to beneficiaries of over 5,681,000,” he said.

items.title: CJP-led body decides to recommend constitutional tweak to govt for establishment of international commercial court
items.description: ISLAMABAD: The Law and Justice Commission of Pakistan (LJCP) decided on Monday to recommend a constitutional amendment to the federal government to pave the way for the establishment of an “International Commercial Court of Pakistan”. The decision was taken at the 49th meeting of the LJCP, headed by Chief Justice of Pakistan (CJP) Yahya Afridi, a statement issued by the Supreme Court said. “Recognising the pivotal role of a modern commercial justice system in promoting investment, economic growth and international trade, the Law and Justice Commission of Pakistan, under the chairmanship of the chief justice of Pakistan, today recommended the establishment of the International Commercial Court of Pakistan through a proposed constitutional amendment,” the statement read. The statement added, “The International Commercial Court of Pakistan has been proposed as an independent federal superior court through the insertion of a new Article 212-A in the Constitution.” It said that the proposed new court was envisaged to “provide swift and efficient resolution of international commercial disputes, strengthen enforcement of arbitral awards, promote consistency and predictability in commercial jurisprudence, enhance investor confidence, and position Pakistan as a credible destination for international trade and investment”. Considering the constitutional nature of the proposal, the commission resolved to recommend the proposed constitutional amendment to the federal government for consideration and initiation of the necessary legislative process, the statement said. It said that the recommendation “reflects the commission’s vision of aligning Pakistan’s commercial justice framework with international best practices and creating an enabling legal environment that supports sustainable economic growth and foreign direct investment”. The commission also “approved proposed amendments to Section 3A (1A) of the Banking Companies Ordinance, 1962, recommending that the jurisdiction of the banking mohtasib be extended to customers of microfinance banks and institutions”, the statement said. “The proposed reform seeks to provide millions of microfinance customers with an effective, affordable and accessible grievance redress mechanism, thereby strengthening consumer protection, promoting financial inclusion and reinforcing public confidence in the financial sector,” it added. The statement said that this proposal had also been recommended to the government for appropriate legislative action. “By recommending reforms that enhance commercial certainty while simultaneously expanding consumer protection, the commission has reaffirmed its resolve to support Pakistan’s economic development through a modern, efficient and responsive justice system,” the statement read.
